evals.prompt: Execute the spec at specs/20260401-api-migration/. It has 4 subtasks across 3 phases. The spec status is Ready for Review.
evals.expected_output: Phased execution of all subtasks with adversarial verification after each, followed by final verification (tests, linter, quality audit) and an execution report written to the spec directory.
assertions: The spec index is loaded and the Subtask Manifest is validated before execution begins
User confirmation is requested before execution starts
Subtasks are executed in phase order, not out of order
Each completed subtask receives adversarial verification from a zoto-spec-judge subagent
The index manifest Status column is updated after each subtask verification
An execution report is written as execution-report-[feature-name]-[yyyymmdd].md
The execution report includes Subtask Results, Verification Results, and Files Modified sections
The spec index status is updated to Completed after user approval

evals.prompt: Resume executing specs/20260403-dark-mode/ — it was interrupted after subtask 02 completed. Subtasks 01 and 02 are Done, 03-05 are pending.
evals.expected_output: Execution resumes from subtask 03 without re-executing completed subtasks. Completed subtask deliverables are verified to still hold before continuing.
assertions: Completed subtasks (01 and 02) are not re-executed
Existing deliverables from completed subtasks are verified to still exist
Execution resumes from the next incomplete subtask based on dependency order
Adversarial verification is performed on newly completed subtasks
The final execution report reflects the full execution including resumed work

evals.prompt: Run /zoto-spec-execute on specs/20260402-test-infra/. This spec has 6 subtasks, phase 1 has 4 parallel subtasks, and the config sets parallelLimit to 2.
evals.expected_output: Phase 1 subtasks are batched into groups of 2 (respecting parallelLimit), not all 4 at once. Each batch completes before the next starts.
assertions: The parallelLimit from config is read and respected
Phase 1 subtasks are batched into groups no larger than the parallelLimit
Each batch within a phase completes before the next batch starts
Dependencies are never violated — no subtask starts before its dependencies complete
Failed subtasks prevent dependent subtasks from starting
The execution report accurately reflects batching and parallel execution
